import { oc } from "@orpc/contract";
import { z } from "zod";
import {
	DaysInputSchema,
	DeveloperCostBreakdownSchema,
	DeveloperDetailsInputSchema,
	DeveloperDetailsSchema,
	DeveloperErrorSchema,
	DeveloperFeatureUsageSchema,
	DeveloperProjectSchema,
	DeveloperSessionSchema,
	DeveloperSessionsInputSchema,
	DeveloperSummarySchema,
	DeveloperTimelineSchema,
	DeveloperTrendDataPointSchema,
	DimensionAnalysisDataPointSchema,
	DimensionAnalysisInputSchema,
	ErrorTrendDataPointSchema,
	ErrorTrendsInputSchema,
	InsightSchema,
	LearningEntrySchema,
	LearningsFeedStatsSchema,
	LearningsTrendDataPointSchema,
	LearningsTrendInputSchema,
	ModelTokensTrendDataSchema,
	OverviewKPIsSchema,
	PaginatedDaysInputSchema,
	ProjectContributorSchema,
	ProjectCostBreakdownSchema,
	ProjectDetailDataSchema,
	ProjectDetailsInputSchema,
	ProjectErrorSchema,
	ProjectFeatureUsageSchema,
	ProjectInvestmentSchema,
	ProjectTrendDataPointSchema,
	RecurringErrorSchema,
	RecurringErrorsInputSchema,
	ROIMetricsSchema,
	ROITrendSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSummaryComparisonSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSummarySchema,
	SessionDetailInputSchema,
	SessionDetailSchema,
	SessionListInputSchema,
	SuccessRateSchema,
	TeamSummaryComparisonSchema,
	UsageTrendDataSchema,
	UserMappingSchema,
} from "./schemas/analytics.js";

export * from "./schemas/analytics.js";

export const HealthSchema = z.object({
	status: z.literal("ok"),
	timestamp: z.number(),
});

export const UserSchema = z.object({
	id: z.string(),
	email: z.string(),
	name: z.string(),
	image: z.string().nullable(),
	activeOrganizationId: z.string().nullable(),
});

export const OrganizationSchema = z.object({
	id: z.string(),
	name: z.string(),
	slug: z.string(),
	logo: z.string().nullable(),
});

export const SessionTagSchema = z.enum([
	"research",
	"new_feature",
	"bug_fix",
	"refactoring",
	"documentation",
	"tests",
	"other",
]);

export const SubagentFileSchema = z.object({
	agentId: z.string(),
	content: z.string(),
});

export const IngestSessionInputSchema = z.object({
	sessionId: z.string(),
	projectPath: z.string(),
	repository: z.string().optional(),
	gitBranch: z.string().optional(),
	gitSha: z.string().optional(),
	tag: SessionTagSchema.optional(),
	content: z.string(),
	subagents: z.array(SubagentFileSchema).optional(),
	organizationId: z.string().optional(),
});

export const IngestSessionOutputSchema = z.object({
	success: z.literal(true),
	sessionId: z.string(),
});

export type IngestSessionInput = z.infer<typeof IngestSessionInputSchema>;

export const contract = {
	health: oc.output(HealthSchema),
	me: oc.output(UserSchema),
	listMyOrganizations: oc.output(z.array(OrganizationSchema)),
	ingestSession: oc
		.input(IngestSessionInputSchema)
		.output(IngestSessionOutputSchema),
	analytics: {
		overview: {
			kpis: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(OverviewKPIsSchema),
			usageTrend: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(UsageTrendDataSchema)),
			modelTokensTrend: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ModelTokensTrendDataSchema)),
			insights: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(InsightSchema)),
			teamSummaryComparison: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(TeamSummaryComparisonSchema),
			successRate: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(SuccessRateSchema),
		},
		developers: {
			list: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(DeveloperSummarySchema)),
			details: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(DeveloperDetailsSchema),
			sessions: oc
				.input(DeveloperSessionsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperSessionSchema)),
			projects: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperProjectSchema)),
			timeline: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperTimelineSchema)),
			features: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(DeveloperFeatureUsageSchema),
			errors: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperErrorSchema)),
			trends: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperTrendDataPointSchema)),
		},
		projects: {
			investment: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectInvestmentSchema)),
			trends: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectTrendDataPointSchema)),
			details: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(ProjectDetailDataSchema),
			contributors: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectContributorSchema)),
			features: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(ProjectFeatureUsageSchema),
			errors: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectErrorSchema)),
		},
		sessions: {
			list: oc
				.input(SessionListInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(SessionAnalyticsSchema)),
			summary: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(SessionAnalyticsSummarySchema),
			summaryComparison: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(SessionAnalyticsSummaryComparisonSchema),
			dimensionAnalysis: oc
				.input(DimensionAnalysisInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DimensionAnalysisDataPointSchema)),
			detail: oc.input(SessionDetailInputSchema).output(SessionDetailSchema),
		},
		roi: {
			metrics: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(ROIMetricsSchema),
			trends: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(ROITrendSchema)),
			breakdownDevelopers: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperCostBreakdownSchema)),
			breakdownProjects: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectCostBreakdownSchema)),
		},
		errors: {
			topRecurring: oc
				.input(RecurringErrorsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(RecurringErrorSchema)),
			trends: oc
				.input(ErrorTrendsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ErrorTrendDataPointSchema)),
		},
		learnings: {
			list: oc
				.input(PaginatedDaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(LearningEntrySchema)),
			stats: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(LearningsFeedStatsSchema),
			users: oc.output(z.array(z.string())),
			projects: oc.output(z.array(z.string())),
			trend: oc
				.input(LearningsTrendInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(LearningsTrendDataPointSchema)),
		},
		users: {
			mappings: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(UserMappingSchema)),
		},
	},
};

    13→		.defaultNow()
    14→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    15→		.notNull(),
    16→});
    17→
    18→export const session = pgTable("session", {
    19→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    20→	expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", {
    21→		withTimezone: true,
    22→		mode: "date",
    23→	}).notNull(),
    24→	token: text("token").notNull().unique(),
    25→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    26→		.defaultNow()

    28→	updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })

    31→		.notNull(),

    34→	userId: text("user_id")
    35→		.notNull()
    36→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    37→	activeOrganizationId: text("active_organization_id"),
    38→});

    41→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),

    43→	providerId: text("provider_id").notNull(),
    44→	userId: text("user_id")
    45→		.notNull()
    46→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    47→	accessToken: text("access_token"),
    48→	refreshToken: text("refresh_token"),
    49→	idToken: text("id_token"),
    50→	accessTokenExpiresAt: timestamp("access_token_expires_at", {

    62→		.notNull(),

    65→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    66→		.notNull(),
    67→});
    68→

## Analysis: ORPC Client Setup and shadcn UI Components in `apps/web`

### 1. ORPC Client (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/lima/apps/web/src/lib/orpc.ts`)

The ORPC client setup is 15 lines total and establishes the RPC connection from the web app to the API.

**How it works:**

- **Line 1-5**: Imports `createORPCClient` from `@orpc/client`, `RPCLink` from `@orpc/client/fetch`, the `ContractRouterClient` type from `@orpc/contract`, `createTanstackQueryUtils` from `@orpc/tanstack-query`, and the shared `contract` type from `@rudel/api-routes`.

- **Lines 7-10**: Creates an `RPCLink` instance configured with:
  - `url`: resolves to `{window.location.origin}/rpc` -- this is a same-origin relative URL, meaning in development Vite's proxy forwards `/rpc` to the API server at `localhost:4010`, and in production it hits the same domain.
  - `fetch`: a custom fetch wrapper that passes `credentials: "include"` to ensure cookies (for authentication sessions managed by `better-auth`) are sent with every RPC request.

- **Line 12-13**: Creates the ORPC client with `createORPCClient(link)`, typed as `ContractRouterClient<typeof contract>`. This gives the client full type safety against the shared contract defined in `@rudel/api-routes`.

- **Line 15**: Creates and exports `orpc` via `createTanstackQueryUtils(client)`. This wraps the ORPC client with TanStack Query utilities, enabling the use of `orpc.someRoute.queryOptions()` or similar patterns in React components with `@tanstack/react-query`.

**Two exports:**
- `client` -- the raw typed ORPC client for direct RPC calls.
- `orpc` -- the TanStack Query-wrapped utilities for use with React Query hooks.

### 2. shadcn UI Component Patterns

The project uses the **unified `radix-ui` package** (version `1.4.3` at `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/lima/apps/web/package.json:24`) rather than individual `@radix-ui/*` scoped packages. All Radix imports use `from "radix-ui"` with named imports like `{ Dialog }`, `{ Slot }`, `{ Label }`, etc.

#### Button (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/lima/apps/web/src/components/ui/button.tsx`)

- Uses `class-variance-authority` (`cva`) for variant management (lines 7-39).
- **Variants**: `default`, `destructive`, `outline`, `secondary`, `ghost`, `link` (lines 11-22).
- **Sizes**: `default`, `xs`, `sm`, `lg`, `icon`, `icon-xs`, `icon-sm`, `icon-lg` (lines 24-32).
- Supports `asChild` prop (line 49) -- when true, renders as `Slot.Root` from `radix-ui` instead of a native `<button>` (line 51).
- Uses `data-slot="button"`, `data-variant`, and `data-size` attributes (lines 56-58).
- Uses the `cn()` utility from `@/lib/utils` for class merging (line 58), which combines `clsx` and `tailwind-merge`.

#### Input (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/lima/apps/web/src/components/ui/input.tsx`)

- Simpler component -- a thin wrapper around native `<input>` (lines 5-19).
- Uses `data-slot="input"` attribute (line 9).
- Applies Tailwind classes for styling including focus-visible ring, aria-invalid states, and dark mode variants (lines 10-14).
- Uses the same `cn()` utility for class merging.

#### Common Patterns Across All UI Components

Looking at the existing UI components in `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/lima/apps/web/src/components/ui/`:
- All components are **function declarations** (not arrow functions).
- All use `React.ComponentProps<...>` for typing props (e.g., `React.ComponentProps<"button">`, `React.ComponentProps<typeof SheetPrimitive.Content>`).
- All apply `data-slot="component-name"` attributes for identification.
- All use `cn()` from `@/lib/utils` for class merging.
- Radix primitives are imported from the unified `"radix-ui"` package with named destructuring (e.g., `{ Dialog as SheetPrimitive }` at `sheet.tsx:2`, `{ Slot }` at `button.tsx:2`).

### 3. Dialog / `@radix-ui/react-dialog` Availability

There is **no** `@radix-ui/react-dialog` in the dependencies. However, the project uses the unified `radix-ui` package (`"radix-ui": "^1.4.3"` at `package.json:24`), which bundles all Radix UI primitives including Dialog. The existing `sheet.tsx` component already demonstrates this: it imports `{ Dialog as SheetPrimitive } from "radix-ui"` at line 2 and uses `Dialog.Root`, `Dialog.Trigger`, `Dialog.Portal`, `Dialog.Overlay`, `Dialog.Content`, `Dialog.Close`, `Dialog.Title`, and `Dialog.Description` (all aliased under `SheetPrimitive`).

import { oc } from "@orpc/contract";
import { z } from "zod";
import {
	DaysInputSchema,
	DeveloperCostBreakdownSchema,
	DeveloperDetailsInputSchema,
	DeveloperDetailsSchema,
	DeveloperErrorSchema,
	DeveloperFeatureUsageSchema,
	DeveloperProjectSchema,
	DeveloperSessionSchema,
	DeveloperSessionsInputSchema,
	DeveloperSummarySchema,
	DeveloperTimelineSchema,
	DeveloperTrendDataPointSchema,
	DimensionAnalysisDataPointSchema,
	DimensionAnalysisInputSchema,
	ErrorTrendDataPointSchema,
	ErrorTrendsInputSchema,
	InsightSchema,
	LearningEntrySchema,
	LearningsFeedStatsSchema,
	LearningsTrendDataPointSchema,
	LearningsTrendInputSchema,
	ModelTokensTrendDataSchema,
	OverviewKPIsSchema,
	PaginatedDaysInputSchema,
	ProjectContributorSchema,
	ProjectCostBreakdownSchema,
	ProjectDetailDataSchema,
	ProjectDetailsInputSchema,
	ProjectErrorSchema,
	ProjectFeatureUsageSchema,
	ProjectInvestmentSchema,
	ProjectTrendDataPointSchema,
	RecurringErrorSchema,
	RecurringErrorsInputSchema,
	ROIMetricsSchema,
	ROITrendSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSummaryComparisonSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSummarySchema,
	SessionDetailInputSchema,
	SessionDetailSchema,
	SessionListInputSchema,
	SuccessRateSchema,
	TeamSummaryComparisonSchema,
	UsageTrendDataSchema,
	UserMappingSchema,
} from "./schemas/analytics.js";

export * from "./schemas/analytics.js";

export const HealthSchema = z.object({
	status: z.literal("ok"),
	timestamp: z.number(),
});

export const UserSchema = z.object({
	id: z.string(),
	email: z.string(),
	name: z.string(),
	image: z.string().nullable(),
	activeOrganizationId: z.string().nullable(),
});

export const OrganizationSchema = z.object({
	id: z.string(),
	name: z.string(),
	slug: z.string(),
	logo: z.string().nullable(),
});

export const SessionTagSchema = z.enum([
	"research",
	"new_feature",
	"bug_fix",
	"refactoring",
	"documentation",
	"tests",
	"other",
]);

export const SubagentFileSchema = z.object({
	agentId: z.string(),
	content: z.string(),
});

export const IngestSessionInputSchema = z.object({
	sessionId: z.string(),
	projectPath: z.string(),
	repository: z.string().optional(),
	gitBranch: z.string().optional(),
	gitSha: z.string().optional(),
	tag: SessionTagSchema.optional(),
	content: z.string(),
	subagents: z.array(SubagentFileSchema).optional(),
	organizationId: z.string().optional(),
});

export const IngestSessionOutputSchema = z.object({
	success: z.literal(true),
	sessionId: z.string(),
});

export type IngestSessionInput = z.infer<typeof IngestSessionInputSchema>;

export const contract = {
	health: oc.output(HealthSchema),
	me: oc.output(UserSchema),
	listMyOrganizations: oc.output(z.array(OrganizationSchema)),
	ingestSession: oc
		.input(IngestSessionInputSchema)
		.output(IngestSessionOutputSchema),
	analytics: {
		overview: {
			kpis: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(OverviewKPIsSchema),
			usageTrend: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(UsageTrendDataSchema)),
			modelTokensTrend: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ModelTokensTrendDataSchema)),
			insights: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(InsightSchema)),
			teamSummaryComparison: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(TeamSummaryComparisonSchema),
			successRate: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(SuccessRateSchema),
		},
		developers: {
			list: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(DeveloperSummarySchema)),
			details: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(DeveloperDetailsSchema),
			sessions: oc
				.input(DeveloperSessionsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperSessionSchema)),
			projects: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperProjectSchema)),
			timeline: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperTimelineSchema)),
			features: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(DeveloperFeatureUsageSchema),
			errors: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperErrorSchema)),
			trends: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperTrendDataPointSchema)),
		},
		projects: {
			investment: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectInvestmentSchema)),
			trends: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectTrendDataPointSchema)),
			details: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(ProjectDetailDataSchema),
			contributors: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectContributorSchema)),
			features: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(ProjectFeatureUsageSchema),
			errors: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectErrorSchema)),
		},
		sessions: {
			list: oc
				.input(SessionListInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(SessionAnalyticsSchema)),
			summary: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(SessionAnalyticsSummarySchema),
			summaryComparison: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(SessionAnalyticsSummaryComparisonSchema),
			dimensionAnalysis: oc
				.input(DimensionAnalysisInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DimensionAnalysisDataPointSchema)),
			detail: oc.input(SessionDetailInputSchema).output(SessionDetailSchema),
		},
		roi: {
			metrics: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(ROIMetricsSchema),
			trends: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(ROITrendSchema)),
			breakdownDevelopers: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperCostBreakdownSchema)),
			breakdownProjects: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectCostBreakdownSchema)),
		},
		errors: {
			topRecurring: oc
				.input(RecurringErrorsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(RecurringErrorSchema)),
			trends: oc
				.input(ErrorTrendsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ErrorTrendDataPointSchema)),
		},
		learnings: {
			list: oc
				.input(PaginatedDaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(LearningEntrySchema)),
			stats: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(LearningsFeedStatsSchema),
			users: oc.output(z.array(z.string())),
			projects: oc.output(z.array(z.string())),
			trend: oc
				.input(LearningsTrendInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(LearningsTrendDataPointSchema)),
		},
		users: {
			mappings: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(UserMappingSchema)),
		},
	},
};

## Summary

Users can now delete organizations if they belong to more than one. When deleting, they can either migrate all sessions to another org using ClickHouse `ALTER TABLE UPDATE`, or permanently delete session data. The API includes ownership verification and cascading deletes via Postgres. The UI adds a danger zone section with a confirmation dialog that shows session counts and migration options.

## Changes

- **API contracts**: Added `getOrganizationSessionCount` and `deleteOrganization` routes
- **Session service**: New `org-session.service.ts` with `getOrgSessionCount`, `deleteOrgSessions`, and `migrateOrgSessions` (fire-and-forget ALTER TABLE UPDATE mutations)
- **API handlers**: Full auth, ownership, and membership verification with cascading org deletion
- **UI components**: shadcn Dialog component + DeleteOrganizationDialog with session migration flow
- **Organization page**: Danger zone section with conditional delete button (disabled if only org)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
